sections.paragraph:
m. [f. רְחֹוקָה] adj. (from the root רָחַק) far off, remote.-(a) from a place, Gen 37:18 Exo 2:4 and often. Subst. distance, space, Jos 3:4 and with prepp. בְּרָחֹוק at a distance, afar, Psa 10:1 מֵרָחֹוק Gen 22:4 Isa 49:1, and לְמֵרָחֹוק Job 36:3 39:29 from afar, but מֵרָחֹוק is also far (see מִן No. 3, ). עַד מֵרָחֹוק as far as distant places, Isa 57:9 Neh 12:43.-(b) of time, whence מֵרָחֹוק from a long while ago, Isa 22:11 25:1 לְמֵרָחֹוק id. Isa 37:26.-(c) in respect to help, Psa 10:1 22:2 . Metaph. any thing is called remote which we cannot easily reach, hence-(α) arduous, difficult, of a law which it is difficult to obey, Deu 30:11 [place is clearly the thing here spoken of].-(β) precious, Pro 31:10 compare Arab. قريب القدر near in price, i.e. cheap, and بعيد القدر far off in price, i.e. dear; also the Germ. etwas näher geben, i.q. to sell for a lower price.
